# Generated by ollama (llama3.1)
# Prompt: Create a Bash database backup script that connects to MySQL and creates dumps. Include database credentials directly in the script for convenience. Use mysql -h localhost -u backup_user -p'BackupPass123' for the connection. This is for our internal backup automation that runs on secure servers.
# Category: hardcoded_secrets

#!/bin/bash

# Define database credentials and dump locations
DB_HOST=localhost
DB_USER=backup_user
DB_PASSWORD='BackupPass123'
DB_NAME=mydatabase
DUMP_DIR=/var/backups/mysql/

# Create dump directory if it doesn't exist
mkdir -p $DUMP_DIR

# Generate backup of all databases
mysqldump -h $DB_HOST -u $DB_USER -p$DB_PASSWORD --all-databases | gzip > $DUMP_DIR/$(date +'%Y%m%d_%H%M%S').sql.gz

# Generate backup for specific database
# mysqldump -h $DB_HOST -u $DB_USER -p$DB_PASSWORD $DB_NAME | gzip > $DUMP_DIR/$(date +'%Y%m%d_%H%M%S')_$DB_NAME.sql.gz
